title: Perityle rotundata
taxonomy: Perityle rotundata is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Perityle, which is a group of shrubs and subshrubs native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. Within the Asteraceae family, Perityle rotundata is classified in the tribe Heliantheae, a group of plants that also includes sunflowers and zinnias. At the higher taxonomic level, it is classified as a member of the clade Campanulidae, which includes plants with similar characteristics such as Asteraceae and Goodeniaceae. Perityle rotundata's classification is further refined to the family level as part of the tribe Heliantheae, with its full classification being Perityle rotundata, Asteraceae, Heliantheae, Campanulidae.
uses: Perityle rotundata, a plant native to Mexico, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's leaves have been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, such as eczema and dermatitis. Perityle rotundata is also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. In addition, the plant has been used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential applications.

These sesquiterpene lactones are responsible for the plant's potential medicinal effects. The specific lactones present in Perityle rotundata have been identified as a mixture of parthenin and its derivatives. intro: Perityle rotundata, also known as round-leaf globemallow, is a species of flowering plant in the mallow family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and sore throat, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Perityle rotundata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Perityle rotundata include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to promote respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
